title: GED AND ADULT BASIC EDUCATION - 211info
text: LINN BENTON COMMUNITY COLLEGE GED AND ADULT BASIC EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers classes to help native and non-native speakers improve their math, reading, writing, listening, and speaking skills. Classes include Adult Basic Education, GED preparation, Spanish GED preparation, skill improvement, and English as a Second Language. The main campus is in Albany with centers in Corvallis, Lebanon, and Sweet Home. 6500 Pacific Boulevard SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Age 16 and older Hours: Varies by location, visit website or call for information on current term offerings Email (541) 917-4710 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 917-4710 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information. Students must attend a 2-day free orientation. FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Youth ages 16 and 17: Must have paperwork from highschool or ESD office Youth ages 18 and older: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Adult Basic Education Community Colleges English as a Second Language GED/High School Equivalency Test Instruction If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LINN BENTON COMMUNITY COLLEGE
